title: Charred Kenyan Sirloin Steak Paella
description: A hearty and flavorful brunch dish from Kenya featuring charred sirloin steak, pickled vegetables, and fluffy rice. Served in a traditional paella pan.

ingredients.name: 1 cup uncooked paella rice
ingredients.notes: Spanish or short-grain rice is best for paella.

ingredients.name: 1 cup mixed pickled vegetables (such as carrots, onions, and bell peppers)
ingredients.notes: Store-bought or homemade, drained and chopped.

ingredients.name: 2 tsp smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: Spanish smoked paprika gives the dish its signature smoky flavor.

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add the sirloin steak and cook until browned, about 2-3 minutes per side. Remove from the pan and set aside.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the smoked paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth and diced tomatoes. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the paella rice to the pan and stir to coat with the tomato mixture.
Return the sirloin steak to the pan, nestling it into the rice mixture.
Add the pickled vegetables, thyme, and cilantro. Stir gently to combine.
Cover the pan with a lid and transfer to the oven. Bake at 375°F for 25-30 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove from the oven and let rest for 5 minutes before slicing the steak and serving with a wedge of lemon.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and crumbled feta cheese, if desired.
